Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Verb phrase with coordinated noun complement
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Trying to pursue two conflicting goals at the same time
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Kughenda na mipango mivili isiyowezekana pamoja
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Linked to traditional goat herding in the Taita hills where divided attention caused livestock loss
First Example Sentence: Mghosi uyo anadima mburi na maghanga kwa kuahidi kila upande jambo tofauti.
Second Example Sentence: Usidime mburi na maghanga wakati wa mkutano wa jamii.
Third Example Sentence: Wanafunzi wengi hudima mburi na maghanga wanapojaribu kazi na masomo bila mpango.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Fighting an imaginary problem or invisible enemy
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Derived from evening shadows cast by large baobab trees in Taita villages
First Example Sentence: Mwanasiasa huyo analwa na kivuri cha mwadi badala ya matatizo ya wananchi.
Second Example Sentence: Usilwe na kivuri cha mwadi ukakosa kuona ukweli.
Third Example Sentence: Watu wa sokoni walicheka waliposikia anapigana na kivuri cha mwadi.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Benefiting secretly from another person’s suffering
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Kunufaika kwa siri kutokana na mateso ya wengine
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Associated with famine-era stories where selfish individuals exploited vulnerable families
First Example Sentence: Mfanyabiashara huyo anakunywa mafu gha ndamu kwa kupandisha bei ya chakula.
Second Example Sentence: Jamii ilimkataa kwa sababu ya kunywa mafu gha ndamu.
Third Example Sentence: Waandishi wa zamani walitumia msemo huo kueleza viongozi dhalimu.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Infinitive clause with object noun phrase
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Silencing someone before they reveal information
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Inspired by trapping noisy birds before dawn in rural homesteads
First Example Sentence: Walijaribu kufunga ndomo ya nyuni kabla ya siri kusambaa.
Second Example Sentence: Usifunge ndomo ya nyuni ikiwa watu wanahitaji ukweli.
Third Example Sentence: Mzee alimwonya kijana dhidi ya kufunga ndomo ya nyuni kwa vitisho.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Following trends blindly without understanding them
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Comes from strong seasonal winds around the Taita highlands that carry loose leaves aimlessly
First Example Sentence: Vijana wengine hugenda na mahewa gha vuya kwenye mitandao.
Second Example Sentence: Usiende na mahewa gha vuya katika maamuzi muhimu.
Third Example Sentence: Alikubali kosa lake la kugenda na mahewa gha vuya bila kuchunguza habari.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Carrying emotional pain or unresolved anger silently
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: The burning ember symbolizes hidden pain carried from one hearth to another
First Example Sentence: Tangu walipotengana amekuwa akibeba gimba ya mwaki moyoni.
Second Example Sentence: Hakutaka kuendelea kubeba gimba ya mwaki baada ya msamaha.
Third Example Sentence: Mashairi ya Taita mara nyingi hutaja kubeba gimba ya mwaki kwa siri.

Idiom: Kurya ndizi mbichi kwa meno gha chuma
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Extended infinitive clause with instrumental phrase
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Attempting an extremely difficult task recklessly
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Bananas are culturally staple foods; eating raw ones with iron teeth exaggerates impossible bravery
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Taita): /kuɾja ⁿdizi ᵐbit͡ʃi kwa meno ɣa t͡ʃuma/
First Example Sentence: Anajaribu kurya ndizi mbichi kwa meno gha chuma kwa kuanzisha biashara bila mtaji.
Second Example Sentence: Usirye ndizi mbichi kwa meno gha chuma ukidhani kila jambo ni rahisi.
Third Example Sentence: Kiongozi huyo alisifiwa kwa kurya ndizi mbichi kwa meno gha chuma wakati wa shida.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Doing work that can never succeed
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in everyday rural activities where woven baskets cannot hold water
First Example Sentence: Kujaribu kumbadilisha bila mazungumzo ni kusomba maji na kikapu.
Second Example Sentence: Mama aliwaambia watoto waache kusomba maji na kikapu.
Third Example Sentence: Walimu walitumia msemo huo kueleza mbinu zisizofaa za kujifunza.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Celebrating too early before success is certain
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Traditional dances before harvest rains were considered risky signs of overconfidence
First Example Sentence: Timu yao ilianza kuhenda ngoma mbere ya mvua kabla ya matokeo kutangazwa.
Second Example Sentence: Usihende ngoma mbere ya mvua ukasahau kazi iliyobaki.
Third Example Sentence: Wazee walimwonya dhidi ya kuhenda ngoma mbere ya mvua.

Idiom: Kufula mwaki kwa maboko gha baridi
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Trying to solve a serious issue without commitment or passion
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Kufanya kazi bila juhudi za kweli
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Fire-making traditions in Taita required warmth and persistence; cold hands symbolized weak resolve
First Example Sentence: Serikali haiwezi kufula mwaki kwa maboko gha baridi katika suala la ajira.
Second Example Sentence: Aliambiwa aache kufula mwaki kwa maboko gha baridi na aanze kuchukua hatua.
Third Example Sentence: Mashirika mengi hushindwa kwa kufula mwaki kwa maboko gha baridi.
